titleStmt.title: SII 25.257: original edition by M. D. Sampath
No. 257. (A.R. No. 257 of 1908.) KURUSPĀL, BASTAR STATE, CENTRAL PROVINCES.

summary.p: On a slab found in the temple ruins in this village. (now at Jagadalpūr)
This Sanskrit record in Nāgarī characters may palaeographically be assigned to the 11th century.

The king is credited with the birudas of the family to which he belonged and they are similar to those found in the Nārāyaṇpāl inscription, with little variations (See, Ep. Ind., Vol. IX, p. 311). This epigraph mentions him as the son of Dhārāvarsha, whose grandson was Kanharadēva. It is probable that Kanharadēva was a heir-apparent at the time of this record.

It refers to a number of countries like Uḍra, Lañji, Ratnapura, Lemṇa, Vēṅgī, Bhadrapaṭṭaṇa and Vajra. But it is not clear whether king Sōmēśvara had conquered kings of these countries as the inscription could not be restored. They seem to be his rivals. Sōmēśvara is credited with the achievements of the killing of the king Madhurāntaka in battle, burning of Vajra country and the capture of six lakhs and 96 villages of Kōsala country.

The inscription records grant of a village (name not clear) but bounded by Indranadī in the south, village Āraṅgā in the east and Kapālika in the west. The name of the village to the north is not clear. The river Indra is the present Indrāvatī, about a mile from Kuruspāl, which flowed through Chakrakūṭa. Chakrakūṭa, the sovereignty of which he acquired has been identified by Hiralal with Chitrakūṭa, which may be a corruption of the older name.
(Published in Ep. Ind., Vol. X, pp. 28-31).

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti sahasraphaṇāmaṇikiraṇa nikarāvabhūsura nāgavaṁśodbhava bhogāvatī
puravareśvara śrīmatkāśyapagotrāṇāṁ . nāgadhvaja vijayaghoṣaṇa prakaṭīkṛta savatsavyā
dhralāṁcchana pradarśanamātra vasīkṛta viśva viśvaṁbharāṇāṁ . Read ‘vaṁśo’mātāṁpitṛ padasevimahe
śvara caraṇa suśrūṣātatparāṇāṁ Read ‘śuśrūṣā’ . vikramākrānta sakalaripunṛpati kirīṭa koṭiprabhā
mayūsvadyotitāmalacaraṇakamalānāṁ . śaktitrayasaṁpaṁnānāṁ . śrī viṁdhyavāsinī devyāḥ The intended reading must be lakṣmyāḥ
prasādāsādita cakrakūṭādhīsvarāgāṁ . kulamalaṁkariṣṇuḥ . sahasra
mā diśaḥ . nikhilāḥ samabhūdvārāvarṣanāmā naresvaraḥ . 1 pautro kanharadevo bhūttasya rājño
nṛpottamaḥ . Astyamitraḥ raṇeśūraḥ suvrāna May be read ‘sutrāta’dvijamitrakaḥ . 2 samastasya praśāṁtasya
nidhānṛpaḥ . śāstā nāgatidevostasamastārirvvu dhaslutaḥ intended reading . 3 tasyāmarṣavibhuktavāṇata
ṣāditārātakaḥ harṣaṁ cātmajane pravarddhayati yaḥ saṁkaṁmalaṁbhūtale karṣatyeva maheśvarasya
syādarśa saṁpūrake dhārāvarṣanareśvaraḥ samamudādamvārivarṣāyataḥ Reading seems to be, ‘samabhavatdṛptāridharṣā’ [.] 4 rājñoguṇḍa
mahādevyāḥ jātaḥ someśvaronṛpaḥ . yogre na vodhavadbaddhayā nasavarṣo nṛpeṇa [saḥ .] 5
. kṣoṇīśoca Perhaps kṣoṇauśacca is intended.kumāra kāṭupagataḥ svāmī May be read ‘sāmrācya’
taḥ . devaḥ śropratigaṁḍabhairava Iti khyātiṁ nṛpālaṁbhanādru drātmeti ca rājabhūṣaṇa Iti
tale . 6 Uḍḍvakarāyamahiṣāgramahesaḥ vīracoḍanṛpasaṁvara kāmaḥ lemṇakā
pagacakro laṁjiratnapurapadmamahebhaḥ . 7 nihatya vīro madhurāṁtakaṁ nṛpaṁ mahāvalastaṁ madhurā
nibhaṁ raṇe tacārdaśtvā nṛpatī nivāsurānkumāra nārāyaṇatāmupāgataḥ . ca na
dītaṭadvaye vibhāti rājahaṁsavat . saveṁgideśakhāṁḍavaṁ vyadīpayatkirīṭavat 6
mapurabhadrapapattanāhārthavajvamaṁpi vajvabhedgakaḥ . vajvasaṁbhava purāṭavīdavobhāti somana ma
hītale . 10 lakṣāṣaṭkaṁ ṣaṇṇāvatyā sametaṁ grāmāṇāṁ yaḥ kosalānāmahārṣīt . vīvicārthaḥ
vicāryo devācāryaḥ sauryamatijasāryaḥ . 11 Evamādiguṇa gaṇālaṁkṛto mahārājādhi rājapara
meśvara parama bhaṭṭārakaḥ śrīsomeśvaradevaḥ kadācidadha prāyaṁ maraṇanimittakaṁ daṁḍaṁ
tona vihita cakrakūṭe Anādiparaṁparayā varttamānaṁ śāśtradṛṣṭyā samavasthitaṁ madvārṣa
nānādeśakāṁ cāhutavānakāṁ syānini t . nāmanī likhyate śrīpati . śrīdhara . ma
śrīmāhava . dhādho goviṁdu . jajā . gāgū . pati rāu . sāve Āhuṇḍa gheṁkṛpu varasūdi
gādyā dhāna . pathaṇāhārāhila . haradeva . kesava . cīkuma . dedū . mādhava . śrīdhara . supaṭu
kallālavāḍo . telivāḍo . kumhāravāḍo . paribhaṭavāḍo . camāravāḍo . cchipāvāḍo
vāḍo . mocivāḍo . kaṁsāravāḍo . Evamādi kānvaṇigvarānbrāhmaṇān Āhūya dattavān para
rataraṁ sthānāṁca śukraṁ nāsti paṭikudavaṁ nāsti Ita Uttaraṁ maye ki kaṭṭavila
naghanapa grāmaḥ pūrvvasyāṁ diśi Āraṁgā . dakṣiṇasyā miṁdranado . pratīcyāṁ kapālika
nigrāmaḥ Etanmadhye yaḥ kaściddeśāṁtaroya nyakaḥ bhāgayuktaḥ kaḥ
tiṣṭati sa sarvodisi madhyavartī tasyāpyayameva dharmaḥ . Atrārthe sākṣiṇaḥ mahāpradhānaḥ ka
mada daṁnāyakaḥ rāmanaḥ saṁdhivigrahināyakaḥ mānavasāha . Adhipatirāutaḥ yasa
na likhitā sarvvaśāstrārnthapāragataṁ duṣutaṁ bhaṭṭopādhyāya śrī someśvara
maṁyo rakṣati tasyāpi mahadeva puṇyaṁ . vahubhirvasudhā . dattā rājabhiḥ sagarādibhiḥ [. yasya ya*]
sya yadā bhūmistasya tasya tadā phalamiti 12 yastu nāiyati sa prayāga vārāṇasyādi
dha brāhmaṇavadhādi pātakaṁ prā vaṁti . svadattāṁ paradattāṁ vāyo hareta vasuṁdharā . ṣaṣṭiṁ varṣa sa
hasrāṇi viṣṭāyāṁ jāyate kṛmiḥ . 13 raṇadhavalarupakāreṇa Utkīritaṁ .
sādhu
Digital edition of SII 25.257 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
